# Novartis to buy U.S.-based biotech firm Excellergy for up to $2 billion | Reuters

Skip to main content

Report AdImage 1

Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionals Learn more about Refinitiv

 - Swiss pharma company Novartis (NOVN.S), opens new tab said on Friday ​it will acquire California-based ‌biotech company Excellergy in a deal worth up ​to $2 billion.

Under the agreement, ​Novartis said it would ⁠pay up to $2 billion ​in upfront and milestone ​payments. The transaction is expected to close in the second ​half of 2026, ​subject to customary conditions, including regulatory ‌approvals.

Keep up with the latest medical breakthroughs and healthcare trends with the Reuters Health Rounds newsletter. Sign up here. [...] The ⁠transaction will strengthen Novartis' presence in the market of immunology in food ​allergy, ​the ⁠company said.

Image 3

Report AdImage 4

The deal comes after Novartis announced ​last week its ​acquisition ⁠of a breast cancer drug candidate for up ⁠to $3 ​billion from U.S. ​biotech firm Synnovation Therapeutics.

# Novartis to buy U.S.-based biotech firm Excellergy for up to $2 billion

By Reuters

March 27, 2026 6:28 AM UTC Updated 24 mins ago

*    
*    
*    

*    
*    
*    
*    
*    

![Image 2: Logo is seen at a building of Swiss drugmaker Novartis in Rotkreuz](https://www.reuters.com/resizer/v2/BSKAJOCQZFIBLDFYOS7B7BMYJA.jpg?auth=04da8b2f135a2ca92bb268c7ac2aa5d3117c84d3d0d6d106bdbb29a7ef314b8f&width=1920&quality=80)

The company's logo is seen at a building of Swiss drugmaker Novartis in Rotkreuz, Switzerland, January 29, 2020. REUTERS/Arnd Wiegmann/File Photo [Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab](https://www.reutersconnect.com/item/logo-is-seen-at-a-building-of-swiss-drugmaker-novartis-in-rotkreuz/dGFnOnJldXRlcnMuY29tLDIwMjY6bmV3c21sX1JDMlpTSkEzVks4WA%3D%3D/?utm_medium=rcom-article-media&utm_campaign=rcom-rcp-lead)

March 27 (Reuters) - Swiss pharma company Novartis [(NOVN.S), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/NOVN.S) said on Friday ​it will acquire California-based ‌biotech company Excellergy in a deal worth up ​to $2 billion.

Under the agreement, ​Novartis said it would ⁠pay up to $2 billion ​in upfront and milestone ​payments. The transaction is expected to close in the second ​half of 2026, ​subject to customary conditions, including regulatory ‌approvals.

Keep up with the latest medical breakthroughs and healthcare trends with the Reuters Health Rounds newsletter. Sign up [here.](https://www.reuters.com/newsletters/reuters-health-rounds/?location=article-paragraph&redirectUrl=%2Fbusiness%2Fhealthcare-pharmaceuticals%2Fnovartis-buy-us-based-biotech-firm-excellergy-up-2-billion-2026-03-27%2F)

The ⁠transaction will strengthen Novartis' presence in the market of immunology in food ​allergy, ​the ⁠company said.

![Image 3](https://aka.clipcentric.com/file/2197270/ad_720x406_p0/1770980162/llhygzKf?f=auto)

Report Ad![Image 4](blob:http://localhost/a2f9abce2c7bd7e72f7c1065e8e25533)

The deal comes after Novartis [announced](https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/novartis-buy-breast-cancer-drug-candidate-synnovation-2026-03-20/) ​last week its ​acquisition ⁠of a breast cancer drug candidate for up ⁠to $3 ​billion from U.S. ​biotech firm Synnovation Therapeutics.

Reporting by Maria ​Rugamer Editing by Dave Graham

PARIS, April 1 (Reuters) - Amundi has ​appointed Claire Cornil ‌as chief operating officer, ​effective ​April 1, Europe's biggest ⁠fund ​manager said on ​Wednesday.

Cornil will also join Amundi’s ​general management ​committee, it said. ‌Cornil ⁠was previously CEO of Societe Generale ​Gestion, ​a ⁠role she held ​since 2023 ​after ⁠joining as deputy CEO ⁠in ​2022.

# China's Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group beats net profit expectations in 2025

By Reuters

March 29, 2026 12:16 PM UTC Updated 5 mins ago

*    
*    
*    

*    
*    
*    
*    
*    

*   Companies

*   [Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Ltd](https://www.reuters.com/company/hansoh-pharmaceutical-group-company-ltd/)Follow   
*   [Glenmark Pharmaceuticals Ltd](https://www.reuters.com/company/glenmark-pharmaceuticals-ltd/)Follow   

SHANGHAI, March 29 (Reuters) - China's Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group [(3692.HK), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/3692.HK), a Roche partner, reported a 27% rise in ​annual profit on Sunday, beating market ‌expectations and boosted by income from innovative medicines and business development deals.

The specialist in oncology, anti-infective, ​central nervous system disease, metabolic disease ​and autoimmune disease drugs has expanded licensing ⁠deals and developed innovative medicines amid ​Beijing's centralised bulk buying programmes, which have ​squeezed generic drug revenues.

Keep up with the latest medical breakthroughs and healthcare trends with the Reuters Health Rounds newsletter. Hansoh said net profit for the 2025 fiscal year that ended December 31 came ​in at 5.56 billion yuan ($804.44 million), well ​above a consensus forecast of 4.97 billion yuan in ‌estimates ⁠compiled by LSEG.

Revenue for the year jumped 22.6% to 15.03 billion yuan, the company said.

In October, Hansoh signed a [licence agreement, opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/chinas-hansoh-pharmaceutical-group-beats-net-profit-expectations-2025-2026-03-29/search/nL6N3VX19N) worth ​up to $1.45 ​billion with ⁠Roche for an investigational treatment of colorectal cancer and other ​solid tumours.

The deal was a part ​of ⁠a string of new licensing agreements last year with other overseas drugmakers including the Swiss ⁠arm ​of India's Glenmark Pharmaceuticals [(GLEN.NS), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/GLEN.NS) ​and Regeneron Pharmaceuticals .

# EU regulator backs Sanofi's injectable version of blood cancer drug

By Reuters

March 27, 2026 1:36 PM UTC Updated 19 mins ago

*    
*    
*    

*    
*    
*    
*    
*    

![Image 2: The logo of French drugmaker Sanofi is seen in Paris](https://www.reuters.com/resizer/v2/GLSQZWM2DNKSHOV3WEELL44IOM.jpg?auth=ff8bd87004625877329b4df39b33606ed61f35b34a86de38daab13e9dc707e1a&width=1920&quality=80)

The logo of French drugmaker Sanofi is seen in Paris, France, January 9, 2026. REUTERS/Gonzalo Fuentes [Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab](https://www.reutersconnect.com/item/the-logo-of-french-drugmaker-sanofi-is-seen-in-paris/dGFnOnJldXRlcnMuY29tLDIwMjY6bmV3c21sX1JDMkxYSUE2UUpHMQ%3D%3D/?utm_medium=rcom-article-media&utm_campaign=rcom-rcp-lead)

*   Companies

*   [Sanofi SA](https://www.reuters.com/company/sanofi-sa/)Follow   

March 27 (Reuters) - French drugmaker Sanofi [(SASY.PA), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/SASY.PA) said on Friday the European Medicines Agency has recommended the ​approval of a subcutaneous version of ‌its blood cancer drug administered through an on-body injector.

Here are the details from the company's statement:

Keep up with the latest medical breakthroughs and healthcare trends with the Reuters Health Rounds newsletter. Sign up [here.](https://www.reuters.com/newsletters/reuters-health-rounds/?location=article-paragraph&redirectUrl=%2Fbusiness%2Fhealthcare-pharmaceuticals%2Feu-regulator-backs-sanofis-injectable-version-blood-cancer-drug-2026-03-27%2F)

*   EMA's ​positive opinion for Sanofi's drug Sarclisa ​was on the basis of late-stage ⁠trials which showed the subcutaneous version demonstrated ​non-inferiority compared with the intravenous formulation. 
*   Sarclisa was first ​approved in the EU in 2020 for treating multiple myeloma, a rare blood cancer that starts in ​the bone marrow, where abnormal white ​blood cells multiply uncontrollably and produce faulty proteins. 
*   If approved ‌in ⁠its new formulation, the drug would be administered via an injector worn on the skin to deliver the medication over time. 
*   Sanofi said the ​European Commission, ​which usually ⁠follows the EMA's recommendation, is expected to make a final decision ​in the coming months. 
*   Sarclisa brought ​in ⁠588 million euros ($677.14 million) in sales in 2025. 
*   The U.S. Food and Drug Administration is also reviewing ⁠the ​company's application for the subcutaneous ​on-body injector version of the drug. 

*   ($1 = 0.8684 euros)
